name: Sylvia's Really Moist Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Use both flavored and plain breads in the stuffing.
2. Mix bread, oats, and seasonings in a large bowl.
3. Remove neck and giblets from turkey; set aside.
4. Melt bacon drippings in a skillet over medium heat until hot.
5. Cook onion in the melted drippings until tender, about 10 minutes.
6. Add butter (not margarine) to the skillet with onions and stir into dry ingredients.
7. Mix cornbread crumbles and sunflower seeds thoroughly into the stuffing mixture.
8. Stuff the turkey tightly.
9. Bake turkey breast side down in a preheated oven until done, about 1 hour.
10. Boil neck and giblets in water with seasonings for about 20 minutes; discard neck and giblets.
11. Add strained broth to any leftover uncooked stuffing.
12. When the turkey is finished cooking, remove it from the roasting pan and add all drippings to the uncooked stuffing.
13. Uncooked stuffing will be very wet. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C) and spray a casserole dish with Pam cooking spray.
14. Fill the casserole dish with stuffing and bake, covered, for 30 minutes.
